steps.title: Find the slope of the given line
steps.content: Rewrite the given equation in slope-intercept form: $$x-8y=12$$ $$-8y=12-x$$ $$y=\frac{1}{8}x-\frac{3}{2}$$ A second line gives no solution with this line if it is parallel but not the same line, so it must also have slope $\frac{1}{8}$ and a different $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Check the keyed choice
steps.content: Choice A is $\frac{1}{2}x-4y=0$. Multiplying by $2$ gives $$x-8y=0,$$ or $$y=\frac{1}{8}x.$$ This line has the same slope as the given line but a different $y$-intercept, so the two lines are parallel and never intersect. Therefore choice A could be the second equation.

steps.title: Note the source ambiguity
steps.content: As written, choices B and C also simplify to lines parallel to the given line with different $y$-intercepts. The keyed answer is A, and A is valid, but the current source text does not make A the only valid choice.
